reasoning: The African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) has been ratified by 47 or 48 of the 54 signatory countries, but Sudan is consistently listed among the seven countries that have not yet ratified it (alongside Benin, Liberia, Libya, Madagascar, Somalia, and South Sudan), with sources confirming this status up to at least January 2025. Sudan has been consumed by a devastating, nationwide civil war since April 2023, leaving it without a functioning standardized legislative body capable of prioritizing and passing an international trade ratification. With the forecast date of June 6, 2025, there are only 25 days remaining before the July 1, 2025 deadline. There has been no recent reporting indicating that the Sovereign Council intends to ratify AfCFTA by decree in the coming weeks. Given the ongoing existential conflict, economic collapse, and complete lack of recent progress on this front, the probability of Sudan ratifying the agreement before July 1, 2025 is extremely close to zero.
